```json
{
  "analysis": "Interesting! O32 with the exclamation mark syntax (='Data'!A1) returned 'Protein_ID', which is correct! This means the exclamation mark syntax works, but the dot syntax doesn't. I need to use the Excel-style sheet reference with exclamation marks instead of dots.

The #N/A errors in O28-O31 suggest that the dot syntax isn't working in LibreOffice Calc. I need to rewrite all formulas using the ! syntax instead.",
  "plan": "I'll rewrite all the formulas using the exclamation mark syntax (='Data'!CellRange) which clearly works in LibreOffice.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 << 'EOF'
from openpyxl import load_workbook
from openpyxl.utils import get_column_letter

wb = load_workbook('protein_expression.xlsx')
ws_task = wb['Task']

print("=== Rewriting all formulas with ! syntax ===")

# Rewrite expression data formulas (C11:L20)
for task_row in range(11, 21):  # Rows 11-20
    for task_col in range(3, 13):  # C-L
        col_letter = get_column_letter(task_col)
        # Use ! syntax: ='Data'!Range
        formula = f"=IFERROR(INDEX('Data'!$D$2:$AZ$201,MATCH($A{task_row},'Data'!$A$2:$A$201,0),MATCH({col_letter}$10,'Data'!$D$1:$AZ$1,0)),\"\")"
        ws_task.cell(task_row, task_col).value = formula

print("Sample formula for C11:")
print(ws_task.cell(11, 3).value)

# Update test formulas with ! syntax
ws_task.cell(28, 15).value = "=INDEX('Data'!$A:$A,2)"
ws_task.cell(29, 15).value = "=MATCH(A11,'Data'!$A:$A,0)"
ws_task.cell(30, 15).value = "=MATCH(C$10,'Data'!$D$1:$AZ$1,0)"
ws_task.cell(31, 15).value = "=INDEX('Data'!$D$2:$AZ$201,1,1)"

wb.save('protein_expression.xlsx')
print("\n=== File saved ===")

EOF
",
      "duration": 1.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}
```
